task: Deep carve wave: drop _rfecv_fit.py / _ensembling_score.py / _phase_composite_post.py further toward their <500/<600/<700 LOC targets by extracting closure-heavy sub-bodies; behavioural-equivalence required because these are real refactors, not pure carves.

_phase_composite_post.py.behavioural_eq_evidence: 65 composite-ensemble + composite-post tests across tests/training/test_composite_ensemble*.py + tests/training/test_composite_post*.py + tests/training/test_phase_composite_post_module_split.py + tests/training/test_monolith_split_w11a_phase_composite_post.py all GREEN post-carve.
_phase_composite_post.py.notes: Cross-target ensemble per-target loop body (~700 LOC inside the nested for loop over original-target names) was lifted to _build_cross_target_ensemble_for_target. Every previously-closure-captured outer local is now passed as an explicit kwarg (~25 kwargs). Mutation contract preserved: helper mutates ``models`` / ``metadata`` / ``_train_pred_cache`` in place via the caller-owned references. The always-None ctx-introspection branches (`if 'ctx' in dir() else None`) at original lines 426 / 433 / 481 -- which evaluated to None because ``ctx`` was never bound inside the function frame -- are preserved verbatim in the sibling so the new path returns the same None for time_ordering / sample_weight / group_ids.

_ensembling_score.py.target_met_reason: Hit 709 LOC (vs <600 target). Three discrete pure-logic blocks safely carved (select_gate_source_split / catastrophic_drop_kn / catastrophic_drop_k2). The remaining body is the ensembling-methods for-loop + diversity/identical/stacking-aware-gate blocks whose state is too deeply interleaved with the level_models_and_predictions / ensembling_methods / ensemble_name / res mutation chain to extract safely without behavioural risk. Further carving would require restructuring around an EnsembleScoreState dataclass which is a real refactor beyond a behavioural-only carve.
_ensembling_score.py.behavioural_eq_evidence: 202 ensembling tests across tests/test_ensembling*.py + tests/training/test_ensembling*.py + tests/training/test_ensemble_*.py + tests/training/test_composite_ensemble*.py + tests/training/test_regression_w*_ensembling*.py + tests/models/test_monolith_split_w11a_ensembling_score.py all GREEN post-carve.
_ensembling_score.py.notes: Three pure helpers extracted: (a) select_gate_source_split -- gate-source preference (OOF first, val/test/train fallback) + coarse-gate threshold flips; (b) catastrophic_drop_kn -- K>2 absolute-target-MAE drop with borderline diagnostic + all-members-catastrophic sentinel; (c) catastrophic_drop_k2 -- K=2 single-member dropout with early-return signal back to caller (since the original returned ``res`` mid-function on this path). All three preserve original side-effects: logger.warning calls on the parent logger name, in-place res-dict stamping, list rebinding, ensemble_name regex-rewrite.

_rfecv_fit.py.target_met_reason: Hit 536 LOC (vs <500 target). The remaining body is the outer while-loop scoring/Optimizer/checkpoint/break-condition state machine, which is tightly coupled to ~12 mutable outer-loop locals (nsteps, prev_score, best_score, best_iter, best_nfeatures, n_noimproving_iters, ran_out_of_time, nofeatures_score, evaluated_scores_mean, evaluated_scores_std, dummy_scores, feature_importances) and emits ``break`` to exit early on six conditions. Lifting the iteration body would require packaging all state in a FitState dataclass AND signaling early-exit via a return enum -- a real refactor whose behavioural-equivalence risk on edge cases (max_runtime_mins, best_desired_score, max_noimproving_iters, consecutive_nan_iters) outweighs the LOC gain. Pre-loop setup + per-fold body carves (the two safe extractions) landed cleanly.
_rfecv_fit.py.behavioural_eq_evidence: 112 RFECV tests across tests/feature_selection/test_biz_val_wrappers_rfecv*.py (90 biz_val tests covering all major param variations + n_features rules + stability_selection + must_include + conditional_permutation + checkpoint + parallel fold dispatch) + tests/feature_selection/test_rfecv_*.py + tests/feature_selection/test_regression_*_rfecv*.py + tests/feature_selection/test_monolith_split_w11a_rfecv_fit.py all GREEN post-carve. The deterministic synthetic-regression behavioural-eq pin (sel.fit() x2 -> identical support_) acts as the regression sensor for future sessions.
_rfecv_fit.py.notes: Two carves: (1) _rfecv_fit_setup.py with 3 pure helpers -- filter_cat_features_by_dtype (drops numeric-encoded cats), resolve_effective_n_jobs (multi-threaded estimator detection + auto-fallback to sequential), resolve_default_scoring (probabilistic_multiclass_error / mean_squared_error defaults). (2) _rfecv_fit_fold.py with _eval_fold_body lifted out of the nested closure -- every previously-captured outer local is now an explicit kwarg (20 readonly locals + 4 mutable containers passed by reference). Parent's _eval_fold becomes a 24-line thin wrapper that forwards into _eval_fold_body. The four mutable containers (scores / feature_importances / fitted_estimators / dummy_scores) keep their reference identity across the parent->sibling boundary so worker-thread GIL-atomic mutations remain observable in the outer loop exactly as before. Removed 6 now-unused imports from parent (sklearn make_scorer, mean_squared_error, is_classifier, is_regressor; mlframe compute_probabilistic_multiclass_error; nullcontext / suppress_stdout_stderr / has_early_stopping_support / get_best_dummy_score / get_feature_importances / split_into_train_test / pack_val_set_into_fit_params / compute_cb_text_processing / CATBOOST_MODEL_TYPES / _detect_multithreaded / clone / hashlib / polars / contextlib).

behavioural_eq_test_summary.monolith_split_sensors: 38 monolith-split sensors GREEN (W11A + W12A across all 3 files)

deviations_from_plan.rationale: Three pure logic blocks carved safely (gate-source-selection 75 LOC + KN-catastrophic 135 LOC + K2-catastrophic 95 LOC = ~305 LOC removed). The remaining body holds the ensembling-methods for-loop + diversity-pair check + identical-collapse + stacking-aware NNLS gate -- these blocks share write-state on ``level_models_and_predictions`` / ``ensembling_methods`` / ``ensemble_name`` / ``_nnls_weights_for_blend`` and the diversity-check block reads + mutates ``_gate_stats`` from the embedded compute_member_quality_gate call. A safe carve would require an EnsembleScoreState dataclass packaging ~15 mutables, which is a real refactor outside the behavioural-only constraint. Per the original task brief: 'Если behavioural-equivalence НЕ achievable... DEFER finding, document precisely в manifest почему. Не ship refactor что меняет numerical output silently.' Deferred.

Pre-loop setup helpers (60 LOC) + per-fold body (~210 LOC) carved cleanly (-270 LOC achieved). The remaining body is the outer while-loop state machine with ~12 mutable outer-loop locals + 6 break conditions + checkpoint persistence + Optimizer.submit_evaluations calls + RAM-aware GC hook. Lifting this requires an OuterLoopState dataclass + an early-exit enum return -- a real refactor whose behavioural-equivalence risk on edge cases (consecutive-NaN guard, max_runtime_mins, best_desired_score, max_noimproving_iters) outweighs the LOC gain. The deterministic Ridge+make_regression sensor in test_monolith_split_w12a_rfecv_fit.py pins the post-carve output for the next session's regression detector.

next_session_recommendations: Consider EnsembleScoreState dataclass refactor for _ensembling_score.py if further LOC reduction is needed; pair with full ensembling biz_value coverage as the behavioural-eq witness.

For _rfecv_fit.py: an OuterLoopState dataclass + EarlyExitReason enum could lift the while-loop body cleanly; the existing biz_value suite (90 tests) is comprehensive enough to serve as the behavioural-eq witness.
All three carves landed under the meta-test no-file-over-1k-LOC sensor (run tests/test_meta/test_no_file_over_1k_loc.py to confirm).
